articles.title: Earlier this week we announced the installation of DJI Dock 3 onto our Team UAV Wildtrak truck. Here are the highlights from the first opera
articles.snippet: Earlier this week we announced the installation of DJI Dock 3 onto our Team UAV Wildtrak truck. Here are the highlights from the first operational flight, which took place at Manchester Barton Aerodrome and was remotely piloted from Glasgow! The system is fully integrated onto the vehicle, allowing us to position, deploy and manage autonomous flights directly within live environments. Missions can be scheduled, triggered as required and operated remotely, with full oversight. This is practical deployment, not concept. It gives us greater flexibility across infrastructure, utilities and complex operational sites, while maintaining consistent and repeatable data capture. The Team UAV truck with Dock 3 is now operational. If you would like more information or a live demonstration to see how this could support your operations, get in touch. hashtag #TeamUAV hashtag #PDG hashtag #Dock3 hashtag #DJI

articles.title: Last week was one of those weeks that really reminds you how far this industry has come. In just five days, our team deployed seven DJI Dock
articles.snippet: Last week was one of those weeks that really reminds you how far this industry has come. In just five days, our team deployed seven DJI Dock systems across three different states, supporting ongoing renewable energy and infrastructure projects that rely on consistent, high-quality geospatial data. Deployments like this go far beyond simply placing hardware in the field. Each site requires careful planning, site surveys, connectivity setup, system testing, and power integration to ensure the docks are ready to operate safely and reliably from day one. Once operational, these systems enable automated daily aerial data collection, providing construction teams with a consistent, real-time view of progress across large and rapidly evolving job sites. Many of these deployments are also fully off-grid, which adds another layer of complexity. That’s where strong partnerships make all the difference. A big thank you to our partners at Aerial Prospex , who provide the off-grid power solutions th

articles.title: “DJI Dock 3: When Winter Freezes, Your Drone Ops Don’t” “Snow, ice, sub‑zero wind… and your drone system is still online.” DJI Dock 3 is bui
articles.snippet: “DJI Dock 3: When Winter Freezes, Your Drone Ops Don’t” “Snow, ice, sub‑zero wind… and your drone system is still online.” DJI Dock 3 is built to keep autonomous drone operations running in harsh environments, not just sunny days. In this alpine deployment, the dock sits directly in the cold – its housing covered in frost – yet it continues to shelter, charge and manage the drone for fully automated missions. That makes it ideal for: Mountain and valley infrastructure inspection (power lines, towers, cable cars, roads). Remote energy sites and pipelines in cold or hard‑to‑reach terrain. Safety and environmental monitoring above snow‑covered slopes and valleys. With DJI Dock 3, teams can launch drones on‑demand or on schedule without sending staff into the elements, getting live video and data from the control room instead. XDRON helps organisations design, deploy and support DJI Dock 3 solutions for rugged, all‑weather operations. hashtag #DJIDock3 hashtag #DroneInABox hashtag #Autonom

articles.title: Detect. Record. Pause. Track. Demonstrating how DJI Dock 3 and FlightHub 2 can enhance remote security operations. https://lnkd.in/druhzquf
articles.snippet: Detect. Record. Pause. Track. Demonstrating how DJI Dock 3 and FlightHub 2 can enhance remote security operations. https://lnkd.in/druhzquf During a pre-programmed waypoint mission, the DJI M4TD used onboard AI recognition to detect the person. FlightHub 2 was set to automatically trigger recording and pause the mission — ready for operator input. From there, tracking was engaged, allowing the drone to track the identified subject with live video streamed in real-time. A powerful workflow for perimeter security, remote monitoring, and public safety missions. Interested in getting started with DJI Dock 3? Reach out to our in-house drone-in-a-box specialists. hashtag #DJIDock3 hashtag #BVLOS hashtag #DroneInABox hashtag #AI hashtag #Security hashtag #PublicSafety hashtag #DJIEnterprise

articles.title: Hardware commands the headlines, but software secures the mission. While the DJI Dock 3 and Matrice 4TD represent formidable engineering, th
articles.snippet: Hardware commands the headlines, but software secures the mission. While the DJI Dock 3 and Matrice 4TD represent formidable engineering, their true operational value for Public Safety is unlocked entirely by FlightHub 2 AIO (All-In-One). This is no longer just a flight management platform; it is the central intelligence hub that turns a static "drone-in-a-box" into a proactive, autonomous network. By integrating directly with the Dock 3 ecosystem, FlightHub 2 AIO delivers: Instantaneous Deployment: Bypasses traditional pilot friction, allowing operators to initiate automated, AI-driven tactical patrols the second an incident is logged. Unified Intelligence: Automatically processes high-resolution sensor data from the M4TD to generate a live-streamed, common operating picture pushed directly to the command centre. Computing & Security: Manages data securely, ensuring sensitive public safety intelligence remains stable, encrypted, and protected. We are no longer simply flying drones; we

articles.title: DJI Dock 3 allows drone missions to run as a complete automated cycle, from take off through to landing and reset. Once a flight is complete
articles.snippet: DJI Dock 3 allows drone missions to run as a complete automated cycle, from take off through to landing and reset. Once a flight is complete, the Matrice 4TD returns to the dock, aligns with the landing platform and lands precisely before the dock closes and the aircraft begins charging ready for the next deployment. The entire process runs automatically, ensuring the system is ready whenever the next mission is required. For organisations introducing autonomous drone capability, reliability at this stage is essential. The ability for the aircraft to return, land and prepare itself for the next flight ensures the system can continue operating without manual intervention. For infrastructure, utilities and industrial environments, this is what allows drone operations to move beyond one off deployments and become part of routine operational oversight. We are continuing to run DJI Dock 3 demonstrations. If you would like to see how autonomous deployment could support your operation, get in

articles.snippet: Our drone dock hybrid vehicle is capable of delivering autonomous aerial capability wherever and whenever it is needed. Integrated with the DJI Dock 3 and soon to be deployed as part of our hashtag #GeoAIR platform, this vehicle will enable 24/7 autonomous drone operations, supporting missions day and night. The system automatically houses, charges, and deploys the aircraft, with missions triggered remotely or scheduled in advance. Live video, telemetry, and captured data are streamed securely to GeoAIR and to authorised client users in real time. This capability is particularly valuable for: 1) Emergency response and rapid deployment scenarios 2) Temporary security coverage at critical or high-risk sites or events 3) Routine infrastructure inspection and asset monitoring 4) Short-term or mobile mapping and survey requirements 5) Situations where permanent dock installation is not practical For our clients and partners, this means autonomous aerial capability can be deployed to a locat

articles.title: DJI Dock 3 – Always‑On Drones for Smarter, Safer Plantations Palm plantations don’t sleep – and neither should your monitoring. DJI Dock 3 t
articles.snippet: DJI Dock 3 – Always‑On Drones for Smarter, Safer Plantations Palm plantations don’t sleep – and neither should your monitoring. DJI Dock 3 turns drones into a 24/7 guardian for fire risks and security across large estates. Palm plantation fire monitoring & security patrols Covering thousands of palm trees with only ground patrols and static cameras leaves blind spots, slow response, and higher risk when something goes wrong. DJI Dock 3 changes this by creating a fully automated drone system that can patrol, detect, and report issues across your plantation day and night. Dock 3 houses and charges a Matrice 4D / 4TD drone, automatically handling take‑off, landing, and data upload, so missions can run on a schedule or be triggered by alarms without a pilot on site. With thermal and zoom cameras, the dock‑powered drone can spot early heat signatures, smoke, or unusual activity between the trees long before ground teams reach the area, turning fire monitoring from reactive response into pro

articles.title: This video shows how DJI Dock 3 drones won’t leave their pre-set flight area — no matter how hard you try! https://lnkd.in/druhzquf We creat
articles.snippet: This video shows how DJI Dock 3 drones won’t leave their pre-set flight area — no matter how hard you try! https://lnkd.in/druhzquf We created custom task areas and GEO Zones in DJI FlightHub 2, then manually attempted to fly the DJI Matrice 4TD beyond these boundaries. Despite our best efforts, the drone simply wouldn’t cross the virtual walls. A great example of how Dock 3 supports safe and controlled operations — especially vital for BVLOS missions and complex environments. hashtag #DJIDock3 hashtag #DJIFlightHub2 hashtag #DroneInABox hashtag #BVLOS hashtag #UAS hashtag #DJIEnterprise

articles.snippet: 24/7 Drone Force Mobile Dock Solutions pair the DJI Dock 3 with the Matrice 4TD drone - engineered seamlessly into a mobile platforms and linked to a compliant, mission-ready drone management suite. articles.title: Live remote drone operations from Africa Energy Indaba in Cape Town! From the conference floor at the Cape Town International Convention Cen
articles.snippet: Live remote drone operations from Africa Energy Indaba in Cape Town! From the conference floor at the Cape Town International Convention Centre, one of our pilots is operating a DJI Dock 3 deployed at a client’s solar PV facility in the Northern Cape. In this video, the aircraft is using the zoom camera to inspect one of the site’s power lines. The dock at this site is primarily used for scheduled thermographic inspections of the solar PV plant. By flying repeatable missions, we are able to identify abnormal temperature differentials that may indicate developing faults before they affect performance. Between these inspections, the same system can also be used to check power lines, substations and other structures across the facility using both high resolution RGB imagery and thermal imagery. Remote drone operations require strict compliance with SACAA regulations. The dock operates within an access controlled area, supported by PTZ cameras for continuous visual oversight, live weather

articles.title: Zipline’s autonomous drone charging stations – now at scale The company shows impressive delivery capabilities across multiple missions. Sin
articles.snippet: Zipline’s autonomous drone charging stations – now at scale The company shows impressive delivery capabilities across multiple missions. Since 2014, starting with medical supply drops in Rwanda, and today, precise urban deliveries with autonomous landing and charging, no human operator needed. Zipline operates in the US, Ghana, Japan and more. Autonomous drones are about to enter hashtag #civilian_airspace, just as they already have in the military domain ✈️ High-level autonomy and supportive regulation are not a question of if, but when. And the answer is: soon
